chapters.title: Konjunktiv II — Hypotheticals
chapters.description: Express wishes, hypotheticals, and polite requests using Konjunktiv II.

lessons.prompt: What does 'Ich würde gerne reisen' mean?
lessons.explanation: 'Würde + infinitive' is the most common Konjunktiv II form. 'Gerne' adds the sense of 'would like to'.

lessons.prompt: Wenn ich reich ___, würde ich die Welt bereisen. (If I were rich, I would travel the world.)
lessons.explanation: 'Wäre' = Konjunktiv II of 'sein'. Wenn + Konjunktiv II → würde + infinitive.

lessons.prompt: Translate: 'Could you help me please?' (polite)
lessons.hint: 'Könnten' = Konjunktiv II of können. More polite than 'Können Sie'.

lessons.prompt: What is Konjunktiv II of 'haben' for 'er'?
lessons.explanation: Key Konjunktiv II forms: wäre (sein), hätte (haben), würde (werden), könnte (können), müsste (müssen).

lessons.prompt: Er ___ das nicht gesagt, wenn er es gewusst hätte. (He would not have said that if he had known.)
lessons.explanation: Past Konjunktiv II = hätte/wäre + past participle. 'Hätte nicht gesagt' = would not have said.

learningMaterial.title: Konjunktiv II — Hypotheticals
learningMaterial.intro: Express wishes, hypotheticals, and polite requests using Konjunktiv II.

concepts.explanation: 'Würde + infinitive' is the most common Konjunktiv II form. 'Gerne' adds the sense of 'would like to'.
'Wäre' = Konjunktiv II of 'sein'. Wenn + Konjunktiv II → würde + infinitive.
'Könnten' = Konjunktiv II of können. More polite than 'Können Sie'.
Key Konjunktiv II forms: wäre (sein), hätte (haben), würde (werden), könnte (können), müsste (müssen).
Past Konjunktiv II = hätte/wäre + past participle. 'Hätte nicht gesagt' = would not have said.

chapters.title: Passive Voice
chapters.description: Use Passiv constructions to describe processes and formal situations.

lessons.prompt: How do you form the Vorgangspassiv (action passive) in German?
lessons.explanation: Vorgangspassiv = werden + past participle. 'Das Buch wird gelesen.' = The book is being read.

lessons.prompt: Das Haus ___ letztes Jahr gebaut. (The house was built last year.)
lessons.explanation: Passive past = wurde + past participle. 'Wurde gebaut' = was built.

lessons.prompt: Translate: 'German is spoken here'
lessons.hint: Passive present: wird + past participle. 'Gesprochen' = past participle of sprechen.

lessons.prompt: What preposition introduces the agent in a passive sentence?
lessons.explanation: 'Von' + dative introduces the agent: 'Das Buch wurde von Goethe geschrieben.' 'Durch' = through/by means of.

lessons.prompt: Es ___ hier nicht geraucht werden. (Smoking is not allowed here — lit. it is not allowed to be smoked here.)
lessons.explanation: Modal + Passiv infinitive: 'darf nicht geraucht werden'. Modal stays conjugated, passive = werden + participle.

learningMaterial.title: Passive Voice
learningMaterial.intro: Use Passiv constructions to describe processes and formal situations.

concepts.explanation: Vorgangspassiv = werden + past participle. 'Das Buch wird gelesen.' = The book is being read.
Passive past = wurde + past participle. 'Wurde gebaut' = was built.
Passive present: wird + past participle. 'Gesprochen' = past participle of sprechen.
'Von' + dative introduces the agent: 'Das Buch wurde von Goethe geschrieben.' 'Durch' = through/by means of.
Modal + Passiv infinitive: 'darf nicht geraucht werden'. Modal stays conjugated, passive = werden + participle.

chapters.title: German Idioms & Register
chapters.description: Sound like a native with idiomatic German expressions.

lessons.prompt: What does 'jemandem auf den Keks gehen' mean?
lessons.explanation: Literally 'to go on someone's cookie' — means to get on someone's nerves.

lessons.prompt: Ich drücke dir die ___! (I'm keeping my fingers crossed for you!)
lessons.explanation: 'Die Daumen drücken' = to cross fingers for someone. Literally 'to press the thumbs'.

lessons.prompt: Translate the idiom: 'That's not my problem' (lit: that's not my beer)
lessons.hint: A colloquial German phrase meaning it's not my concern/not my business.

lessons.prompt: What does 'auf dem Holzweg sein' mean?
options: To be on the wrong track
lessons.correctAnswer: To be on the wrong track
lessons.explanation: Literally 'to be on the log road' — means to be mistaken or on the wrong path.

lessons.prompt: Das geht mir ___ den Rücken runter. (That gives me chills / That's spine-tingling.)
lessons.explanation: 'Den Rücken runtergehen' = literally goes down the back — used for both positive (thrilling) and negative (creepy) chills.

learningMaterial.title: German Idioms & Register
learningMaterial.intro: Sound like a native with idiomatic German expressions.

concepts.explanation: Literally 'to go on someone's cookie' — means to get on someone's nerves.
'Die Daumen drücken' = to cross fingers for someone. Literally 'to press the thumbs'.
A colloquial German phrase meaning it's not my concern/not my business.

concepts.term: To be on the wrong track
concepts.explanation: Literally 'to be on the log road' — means to be mistaken or on the wrong path.
'Den Rücken runtergehen' = literally goes down the back — used for both positive (thrilling) and negative (creepy) chills.

chapters.title: Extended Participial Phrases
chapters.description: Use extended participial modifiers — a hallmark of formal German writing.

lessons.prompt: What does an extended participial phrase replace in formal German?
lessons.explanation: Extended participial phrases compress relative clauses: 'der gestern angekommene Zug' = 'der Zug, der gestern angekommen ist'.

lessons.prompt: Der von ihm ___ Brief war sehr höflich. (The letter written by him was very polite.)
lessons.explanation: Participial phrase: 'der von ihm geschriebene Brief'. The past participle gets adjective ending.

lessons.prompt: Translate using an extended participial phrase: 'The currently running experiment'
lessons.hint: 'Laufend' = running (present participle). Gets adjective ending: laufende.

lessons.prompt: What form does a present participle take in German?
lessons.explanation: Present participle = infinitive + d: laufen → laufend, arbeiten → arbeitend, schreiben → schreibend.

lessons.prompt: Die in diesem Bericht ___ Daten sind nicht aktuell. (The data presented in this report are not current.)
lessons.explanation: Past participial phrase with adjective ending. 'Die präsentierten Daten' = the presented data.

learningMaterial.title: Extended Participial Phrases
learningMaterial.intro: Use extended participial modifiers — a hallmark of formal German writing.

concepts.explanation: Extended participial phrases compress relative clauses: 'der gestern angekommene Zug' = 'der Zug, der gestern angekommen ist'.
Participial phrase: 'der von ihm geschriebene Brief'. The past participle gets adjective ending.
'Laufend' = running (present participle). Gets adjective ending: laufende.
Present participle = infinitive + d: laufen → laufend, arbeiten → arbeitend, schreiben → schreibend.
Past participial phrase with adjective ending. 'Die präsentierten Daten' = the presented data.
